pi_items.metadata_value: GCompris Gravity activity with projectile physics
pi_items.verified_value: GCompris has a 'Gravity' activity, but it is a spaceship-dodging game with left/right controls, not a projectile physics simulator
pi_items.source: GitHub KDE/gcompris repository - gravity activity source code (Gravity.qml, gravity.js)

pi_items.metadata_value: Fire projectile with Angle and Velocity sliders; hit target object
pi_items.verified_value: GCompris Gravity activity uses directional keyboard controls (left/right arrows) to move a spaceship. Players avoid falling planets using gravity arrows as guides. No projectile firing, no angle/velocity sliders, no target-hitting mechanics.
pi_items.source: GitHub KDE/gcompris repository - gravity activity source code analysis

pi_items.metadata_value: GCompris has 180+ activities including a Gravity activity with physics-based projectile interaction
pi_items.verified_value: GCompris-qt (modern version, ~190 activities) does include a Gravity activity, but it is fundamentally different from what the task describes. The actual activity is a spaceship navigation game under gravity effects, not a projectile motion/ballistics simulator.
pi_items.source: Ubuntu package information, GitHub KDE/gcompris repository, activity source code verification

pi_items.metadata_value: Science category, Gravity activity, projectile physics
pi_items.verified_value: GCompris Gravity activity exists and is in the Science category, but teaches gravity concepts through spaceship navigation, not through projectile physics or ballistic calculations.
pi_items.source: GitHub repository structure and source code analysis
Metadata only - no actual screenshot data to verify

privileged_info_summary: The task 'projectile_physics_gravity' describes an activity that does not exist in GCompris. While GCompris does contain a real 'Gravity' activity in its Science category, the actual activity is a spaceship-dodging game using left/right directional controls to avoid falling planets under gravitational effects. The task incorrectly describes a projectile physics simulator with Angle and Velocity sliders for hitting target objects, which is not part of the real GCompris Gravity activity. The task data appears to be entirely synthetic - a fictional activity description that does not correspond to any real GCompris functionality.
